skill.name: documentation
skill.description: Documentation workflow for planning, generating, and maintaining project docs, API references, architecture notes, READMEs, and guides.
skill.summary: Plan and produce complete software documentation across APIs, architecture, onboarding, release notes, and maintenance tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown skill names, fenced prompts, and ordinary documentation words. No executable code, network access, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Skill References
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code and fenced copy-paste prompts that name other documentation skills. They are not shell, Ruby, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a prose workflow document, and the cited lines contain Markdown formatting or skill names. No command interpreter, script file, or user input execution path exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections come from plain documentation text such as skill descriptions, design steps, guides, and authentication topics. No cryptographic algorithm or implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation planning content and headings, not code. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms in Documentation Tasks
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections refer to a Mermaid diagram skill and a checklist item for validating links. These are normal documentation maintenance tasks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation workflow content, not host inspection commands. No filesystem enumeration, network probing, or environment discovery behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Software teams often lack a clear path for complete and current documentation. This skill provides a structured workflow for APIs, architecture, READMEs, wikis, changelogs, and maintenance.
actual_capabilities: Plans documentation structure before writing content.
Guides API reference and OpenAPI specification creation.
Organizes architecture documentation with C4 and Mermaid diagram workflows.
Supports README, tutorial, onboarding, wiki, changelog, and release note workflows.
Defines quality gates for documentation completeness and maintenance.
limitations: Does not generate documentation by itself without the referenced companion skills.
Does not inspect code automatically or validate generated examples.
Does not publish documentation sites or configure hosting directly.
Does not include project-specific style rules unless the user provides them.

use_cases.title: Create Developer Documentation
use_cases.description: Build a structured documentation plan for APIs, setup steps, examples, and code reference material.

use_cases.title: Document System Architecture
use_cases.description: Coordinate architecture overviews, C4 diagrams, Mermaid diagrams, data flows, and deployment notes.

use_cases.title: Maintain Team Knowledge Bases
use_cases.description: Organize onboarding guides, wiki pages, process docs, changelogs, and review checklists.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Documentation Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan the documentation structure for my project. Include README, setup, API, architecture, and maintenance sections.

prompt_templates.title: Create API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to prepare API documentation for my service. Include endpoint reference, authentication notes, request details, response details, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Build Architecture Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to document my system architecture. Include context, container, component, sequence, data flow, and deployment documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to review my existing documentation. Identify outdated sections, missing guides, weak examples, broken links, and release note gaps.

output_examples.input: A team needs documentation for a new developer platform.
output: A documentation map covering README, setup, API reference, architecture, and troubleshooting.
A prioritized writing plan with owners and quality gates.
A maintenance checklist for links, examples, diagrams, and release notes.

output_examples.input: An API service needs public reference documentation.
output: Endpoint groups with authentication, parameters, responses, and error handling sections.
Suggested OpenAPI specification tasks and usage example requirements.
Review checks for accuracy, completeness, and working examples.

output_examples.input: An engineering manager wants a better onboarding wiki.
output: A wiki structure for setup, team processes, system overview, runbooks, and FAQs.
Page creation prompts for onboarding and operational knowledge.
A recurring review process for stale pages and missing ownership.

best_practices: Start with audience, scope, and documentation type before drafting content.
Keep examples, diagrams, and links under regular review.
Use quality gates to confirm completeness before publishing.
anti_patterns: Generating large documents before defining readers and goals.
Publishing API docs without verified examples and error cases.
Treating documentation as complete without assigning maintenance ownership.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps plan and coordinate API docs, architecture docs, READMEs, code docs, tutorials, wikis, changelogs, and release notes.

faq.question: Does it read my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompts. Code inspection depends on the AI tool and any companion skills you use.

faq.question: Can it create OpenAPI specifications?
faq.answer: It guides OpenAPI specification work through the related OpenAPI workflow step.

faq.question: Is this useful for architecture documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes C4, Mermaid, data flow, sequence, deployment, and system overview documentation steps.

faq.question: Does it publish documentation sites?
faq.answer: No. It includes planning for documentation sites, but publishing and hosting require separate tools or manual setup.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains Markdown guidance only, with no executable code, network calls, or secret handling.
